Get Your Solar Proposal

Sun Direct Renewable - Alberta

How a Grid-Connected Solar System Works

Solar panels convert daylight into electricity for your property. An inverter makes that energy usable, while monitoring helps you understand performance.

Home/How a Grid-Connected Solar System Works

From sunlight to usable power

Panels produce direct-current electricity. An inverter converts it to alternating current for your home, business or farm.

Use, export and monitor

Your property uses solar electricity as it is produced. Depending on approvals and arrangements, surplus may flow to the grid and appear as a credit.

Design matters

Orientation, shading, weather, equipment, losses and actual electricity use all affect the annual result. A professional model explains its assumptions.

Frequently asked questions

What to know about solar

Does my property use solar power before electricity from the grid?+

A grid-connected property generally uses solar electricity as it is produced, with the grid supplying additional power when demand is higher than solar production.

What does the inverter do?+

Panels produce direct-current electricity. The inverter converts it into alternating current that the property can use and also provides operating and monitoring information.

Why does actual production change from month to month?+

Day length, weather, temperature, snow, shading and household or business conditions vary throughout the year. Annual estimates are more useful than expecting equal monthly production.

Can I see how much electricity my panels are producing?+

Most modern systems include monitoring. The exact information and access depend on the selected inverter and platform, and monitoring should be set up during commissioning.

Does the system need regular manual operation?+

Normally, no. A commissioned grid-connected system operates automatically, while monitoring helps identify unusual performance or equipment alerts that may need attention.

Your next step

Turn Sunlight Into a Smarter Energy Plan

Share your electricity usage and property details. We’ll organize the information needed to shape a clear solar proposal.
